word_list.word: a whistling woman and a crowing hen are neither fit for God nor men
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ wˈɪslɪŋ wˈʊmən ænd ɐ kɹˈoʊɪŋ hˈɛn ɑːɹ nˈiːðɚ fˈɪt fɔːɹ ɡˈɑːd nˈɔːɹ mˈɛn

senses.definition: used to imply that women who engage in behaviors that are traditionally associated with men or masculinity are inappropriate and unworthy of respect or acceptance

word_list.word: a woman's place is in the home
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ wˈʊmənz plˈeɪs ɪz ɪnðə hˈoʊm

senses.definition: used to imply that women's primary role is within the household, and that their most valuable contribution to society is as a wife and mother

examples.x: The old-fashioned father insisted that his daughter quit her job and stay home to take care of the family, stating that a woman's place is in the home.

word_list.word: a woman's work is never done
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ wˈʊmənz wˈɜːk ɪz nˈɛvɚ dˈʌn

senses.definition: used to imply that women have an endless list of responsibilities and duties that often go unnoticed or undervalued, and that they are expected to prioritize others over themselves

word_list.word: {not} choose {one's} women or {one's} linen by candlelight
word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt tʃˈuːz wˈʌnz wˈɪmɪn ɔːɹ wˈʌnz lˈɪnɪn baɪ kˈændəllˌaɪt

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is essential to see things clearly without any illusions or romanticized ideas before making a significant decision

examples.x: Before accepting a job offer, it's important to visit the workplace and meet your potential coworkers to get a feel for the company culture and work environment, following the proverb never choose your women or your linen by candlelight.

word_list.word: daughters and dead fish are not keeping wares
word_list.pronunciation: dˈɔːɾɚz ænd dˈɛd fˈɪʃ ɑːɹ nˌɑːt kˈiːpɪŋ wˈɛɹz

senses.definition: used to imply that a daughter's worth lies only in her ability to get married and that she is a burden on her family if she remains unmarried

examples.x: I overheard my neighbor telling her daughter that daughters and dead fish are not keeping wares, and it made me realize how deeply ingrained these outdated beliefs still are in our society.

word_list.word: England is the paradise of women, the hell of horses and the purgatory of servants
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɪŋɡlənd ɪz ðə pˈæɹədˌaɪs ʌv wˈɪmɪn ðə hˈɛl ʌv hˈɔːɹsᵻz ænd ðə pˈɜːɡətˌoːɹi ʌv sˈɜːvənts

senses.definition: used to imply women live luxuriously in England, while horses are mistreated and overworked, and domestic servants lead a challenging life

word_list.word: female of the species is more deadly than the male
word_list.pronunciation: fˈiːmeɪl ʌvðə spˈiːsiːz ɪz mˈoːɹ dˈɛdli ðɐn ðə mˈeɪl

senses.definition: used to suggest that women are more dangerous or aggressive than men

examples.x: He always warned his friends to be cautious of women, saying that the female of the species is more deadly than the male and that they should never underestimate a woman's ability to manipulate and deceive.

word_list.word: hell hath no fury like a woman scorned
word_list.pronunciation: hˈɛl hæθ nˈoʊ fjˈʊɹi lˈaɪk ɐ wˈʊmən skˈɔːɹnd

senses.definition: used to suggest that a woman's anger and retaliation can be intense and powerful, and that it is unwise to underestimate or provoke her in such circumstances

examples.x: After John cheated on her, Mary's anger was so intense that she truly embodied the saying 'hell hath no fury like a woman scorned'.

word_list.word: old maids lead apes in hell
word_list.pronunciation: ˈoʊld mˈeɪdz lˈiːd ˈeɪps ɪn hˈɛl

senses.definition: used to insult unmarried women by suggesting that they are inferior, undesirable, and destined for a life of misery and punishment in the afterlife

word_list.word: one tongue is enough for a woman
word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌn tˈʌŋ ɪz ɪnˈʌf fɚɹə wˈʊmən

senses.definition: used to suggest that women talk too much or are too outspoken and that they should be more restrained in their speech

examples.x: The old man chided his wife, saying that one tongue is enough for a woman and it should be used for less talking and more listening.

word_list.word: silence is a woman's best garment
word_list.pronunciation: sˈaɪləns ɪz ɐ wˈʊmənz bˈɛst ɡˈɑːɹmənt

senses.definition: used to imply that a woman's virtue and beauty are best demonstrated by her silence

examples.x: The old saying goes that silence is a woman's best garment, but I don't agree with that.

word_list.word: a tidy house holds a bored woman
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ tˈaɪdi hˈaʊs hˈoʊldz ɐ bˈoːɹd wˈʊmən

senses.definition: used to suggest that a woman who focuses solely on maintaining a clean house may not be engaging in more stimulating or fulfilling activities

examples.x: Lisa's sister, an artist, always had a cluttered studio filled with paintings and sculptures; she would laugh and say that a tidy house holds a bored woman.
Sarah's friends noticed that she had become a bit too obsessed with cleaning since she quit her job; they joked that a tidy house holds a bored woman.

word_list.word: the gray mare is the better horse
word_list.pronunciation: ðə ɡɹˈeɪ mˈɛɹ ɪz ðə bˈɛɾɚ hˈɔːɹs

senses.definition: used to imply that sometimes a woman can be more skilled, capable, or better than a man, emphasizing that gender does not determine one's abilities or qualities

examples.x: Many people assume that men are naturally better at sports, but time and time again, female athletes have proven that the gray mare is the better horse, often outperforming their male counterparts.

word_list.word: the way to a man's heart is through his stomach
word_list.pronunciation: ðə wˈeɪ tʊ ɐ mˈænz hˈɑːɹt ɪz θɹuː hɪz stˈʌmək

senses.definition: used to suggest that cooking and serving food that a man enjoys is a way to win his affection and love

examples.x: Jane believed that the easiest way to win her crush's heart was to cook him his favorite meal, as the old saying goes, 'the way to a man's heart is through his stomach'

word_list.word: six hours' sleep for a man, seven for a woman, and eight for a fool
word_list.pronunciation: sˈɪks ˈaɪʊɹz slˈiːp fɚɹə mˈæn sˈɛvən fɚɹə wˈʊmən ænd ˈeɪt fɚɹə fˈuːl

senses.definition: used to suggest that men require less sleep than women, and anyone who sleeps for more than eight hours is foolish

word_list.word: a man is as old as he feels, (and|) a woman as old as she looks
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ mˈæn ɪz æz ˈoʊld æz hiː fˈiːlz ænd ɐ wˈʊmən æz ˈoʊld æz ʃiː lˈʊks

senses.definition: used to suggest that women are primarily valued for their beauty and youth, while men are valued for their experience and wisdom

word_list.word: {not} [reveal] a man's wage and (a|) woman's age
word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt ɹɪvˈiːl ɐ mˈænz wˈeɪdʒ ænd ɐ wˈʊmənz ˈeɪdʒ

senses.definition: used to imply that it is  impolite or inappropriate to disclose a man's salary or a woman's age in public

examples.x: As a general rule, it is considered impolite to ask someone about their salary or age, as the proverb goes, 'never reveal a man's wage and woman's age'.

word_list.word: good men are scarce
word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈʊd mˈɛn ɑːɹ skˈɛɹs

senses.definition: used to suggest that men who possess desirable qualities such as honesty, kindness, and reliability are not common or easy to find

examples.x: Many women believe that good men are scarce and that it is difficult to find a partner who meets their standards for character and personality.

word_list.word: boys will be boys
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɔɪz wɪl biː bˈɔɪz

senses.definition: used to excuse or dismiss negative behavior by boys or men, with the implication that it is simply part of their nature

examples.x: When the boys started playing soccer inside the house and broke the vase, his mother just sighed and said, "boys will be boys."
Although their pranks were annoying, their father shrugged and said, "boys will be boys" when they got into trouble.
They always get into mischief, but she just laughs and says, "boys will be boys" when her sons act out.
The boys' roughhousing didn't surprise anyone at the camp; after all, boys will be boys.
Instead of scolding them, the coach said, "boys will be boys," after they ran around the field at full speed.

word_list.word: children should be seen and not heard
word_list.pronunciation: tʃˈɪldɹən ʃˌʊd biː sˈiːn ænd nˌɑːt hˈɜːd

senses.definition: used to imply that children should remain quiet and obedient in the presence of adults, and that their voices and opinions are not valuable or deserving of respect

examples.x: The strict grandmother scolded her granddaughter for speaking out of turn, saying that children should be seen and not heard to emphasize the importance of obedience and respect for elders.

word_list.word: words are for women, actions for men
word_list.pronunciation: wˈɜːdz ɑːɹ fɔːɹ wˈɪmɪn ˈækʃənz fɔːɹ mˈɛn

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is men who are known for honoring their word, not women

word_list.word: girls will be girls
word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɜːlz wɪl biː ɡˈɜːlz

senses.definition: used to suggest that certain behavior or actions, such as being emotional or talkative, are typical or expected of girls or women

examples.x: She's always been a bit dramatic, but I guess girls will be girls.
